acceptance_targets: [ch08_f001_sanitization_abstraction] PASS if the revised passages at lines 103, 297, and 309 each end on an observable action/sensation or unresolved interpersonal risk, and none of those lines rely on abstract closure labels such as "line item," "contract," or "leverage"; FAIL otherwise.

[ch08_f004_boundary_lexicon_overliteral] PASS if the revised post-line-109 passages communicate the same limits without repeatedly naming them as "boundary/terms/contract/leverage" in more than two lines total, and the reader can still infer each operative rule from dialogue/action; FAIL otherwise.
[FBR-C01-010] Pass if the revised line uses a distinct bodily cue tied to this scene and removes throat-tightening language; fail if the same somatic template remains in the cited span.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at lines 103, 297, and 309 each end on an observable action/sensation or unresolved interpersonal risk, and none of those lines rely on abstract closure labels such as "line item," "contract," or "leverage"; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: At emotional hinge points, the narration converts embodied consequence into administrative abstraction ("line item checked," "contract," "leverage"), which functions as sanitization drift by defusing scene volatility right after confession/intimacy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:103, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:297, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:309 so each beat stays in concrete aftermath (body sensation, gesture, social risk, or immediate behavioral choice) instead of interpretive summary nouns. Keep uncertainty active rather than adjudicating what the night 'means.'

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised post-line-109 passages communicate the same limits without repeatedly naming them as "boundary/terms/contract/leverage" in more than two lines total, and the reader can still infer each operative rule from dialogue/action; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: Mechanical over-reuse of style-bible-aligned stress vocabulary ("boundary," "terms," "contract," "leverage") begins to sound generated from token lists instead of evolving scene language, reducing tonal range.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the explicit boundary setup in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:55 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:59, but revise later repeats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:259, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:273, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:297, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:309 into behavior-level or image-level language that implies terms without repeating the same lexical set.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised line uses a distinct bodily cue tied to this scene and removes throat-tightening language; fail if the same somatic template remains in the cited span.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book somatic pattern: 'throat tightened/closed' appears in 4 chapters (chapter_02, chapter_07, chapter_08, chapter_12). This chapter's instance should REPLACE to avoid mantra diction during intimacy-boundary negotiation.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2524-2528, replace 'Marisol's throat tightened' with intimacy-specific embodiment (breath hitch, hand tremor against fabric, stomach drop, pulse flare) tied to the consent-and-boundary exchange.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When choosing between reputationally safe abstraction and specific discomfort, choose specificity. Render addiction, sexuality, deceit, class shame, and grief at lived scale. In edge cases, preserve character truth and causal accountability over reader comfort or institutional decorum.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene demands bodily, moral, or economic specificity, euphemistic phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with concrete stakes and observable consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; full forms appear only for emphasis, ritual language, or character-specific formal posture

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density and pressure modes: pair high-conflict chapters with quiet domestic or documentary chapters, vary dialogue-to-interiority ratios, and prevent identical chapter skeletons in consecutive sequence.
prose_style_profile.diction: contemporary literary diction with class-aware, borough-specific concreteness; avoid prestige-neutral workshop language
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Marisol, with rare brief distance expansions for institutional scenes
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternating tensile compression and reflective extension; line-level cadence should carry emotional pressure, not just information
